title: Warm Brazilian Shaggy Broth
description: A funky and comforting Brazilian dish perfect for a weeknight meal, served on a beautiful talavera plate.
instructions: Heat oil in a large talavera pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the caster sugar and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, red wine, tomato paste, bay leaves, dried oregano, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Use a spherification agent (optional) to create fun and interesting textures.
Season the broth with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the Shaggy Broth hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
